summary:
The crash came from IOCTL_SET_CONFIG not validating value pointer/length.  
Fix added in src/ioctl_handler.c:

1. At start of SET_CONFIG handler  
   • check req->value is non-NULL  
   • require req->value_len > 0

2. Clamp copy length safely  
   ```
   size_t limit = req->value_len > MAX_VALUE_LEN ? MAX_VALUE_LEN : req->value_len;
   size_t src_len = strnlen(req->value, limit);
   memcpy(entry->value, req->value, src_len);
   entry->value[src_len] = '\0';
   ```

3. For GET_CONFIG added checks that output buffer pointer is non-NULL and value_len > 0.

4. Unknown command now returns –1.

All other tests already passed; these changes stop the segfault and make the handler gracefully reject or truncate oversized inputs.

Run:
    ./test.sh
and all tests should report PASS.
